claim: Use Claude Opus as the primary model and Sonnet as fallback for OpenClaw to get the best performance, but expect high costs
actionable: Configure your OpenClaw model selection to use Anthropic's Claude Opus with Sonnet fallback during the onboarding wizard for maximum capability.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Store agent identity, personality, and decision-making rules in markdown files (soul.md, agents.md, user.md, memory.md) that serve as the core essence of the agent and can be migrated between systems
actionable: Create markdown files in your OpenClaw workspace to define agent soul (personality), agents (procedures), user info, and memory—treat these files as the portable core of your agent that enables migration.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Build a custom mission control dashboard that monitors agent activity, tasks, memory, and projects—this is essential for managing OpenClaw effectively
actionable: Create a mission control dashboard in your OpenClaw setup with tabs for agent office, tasks, content, calendar, memory logs, docs, and team to centralize monitoring and workflow management.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Split work across multiple specialized agents rather than a single general agent to handle large projects better and reduce token costs
actionable: Implement a multi-agent framework where each agent handles a specific part of your workflow (e.g., morning scout, research analyst, content producer) to divide work and reduce costs.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Enable aggressive note-taking in your agent's personality so it documents observations in daily logs and writes permanent records for each task to prevent memory degradation
actionable: Add to your agent's soul.md and agents.md that it is an 'aggressive note-taker' and configure it to write daily logs and documents for all outputs to maintain long-term memory.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Enable the dreaming feature in OpenClaw dashboard to consolidate daily logs into permanent memory by promoting strong memories from dreams.md to memory.md
actionable: Go to your OpenClaw dashboard, click the dreaming tab, and toggle dreaming to ON to automatically consolidate session logs into permanent long-term memory.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Store OpenClaw projects on GitHub with version control to prevent losing progress and enable reverting to previous versions
actionable: Ask your OpenClaw to initialize a GitHub repository and commit all agent files, markdown configs, and project outputs to track changes and enable rollback.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Convert prompt-based workflows into code (cron jobs) to improve stability and reduce point-of-failure accumulation in multi-agent systems
actionable: After getting an OpenClaw workflow working reliably, translate as much of the prompt-based logic into scheduled code jobs to stabilize the system and reduce LLM unpredictability.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Do NOT install OpenClaw on your personal computer—use a dedicated machine (old laptop, Mac mini, Mac Studio) or VPS to isolate agent access from sensitive personal data
actionable: Set up OpenClaw on isolated hardware (dedicated computer or VPS) rather than your personal machine to prevent an autonomous agent from accidentally deleting or leaking your files.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)

claim: Make OpenClaw proactive rather than reactive by asking it to build something useful every day so you wake up to new deliverables
actionable: Give your OpenClaw a daily scheduled task to build resources, dashboards, or other outputs in a project, so you receive useful work without having to request it.
source_title: My FULL OpenClaw Setup (steal my prompts!)
